title: About This Module: Values and Principles
overview:
Welcome to Module 1: The Values and Principles of the UK.

This module covers the fundamental principles of British life and what it means to become a permanent resident or citizen. You will learn about the responsibilities and freedoms that come with living in the UK, the process of becoming a permanent resident, and how the Life in the UK test works.

Even though this module is an introduction, the test questions can be drawn from it — so make sure you learn all the facts here, including the details of the application process.

study_sections.heading: What You Will Learn
study_sections.content:
In this module, you will learn about:
• The fundamental principles of British life
• The responsibilities and freedoms of all UK residents
• How to become a permanent resident or citizen
• The format of the Life in the UK test

Britain is a modern, thriving society with a long and illustrious history. Our people have been at the heart of the world's political, scientific, industrial and cultural development. The UK is proud of its record of welcoming new migrants who add to the diversity and dynamism of national life.

Applying to become a permanent resident or citizen is an important decision and commitment. You will be agreeing to accept the responsibilities that come with permanent residence and to respect the laws, values and traditions of the UK.

study_sections.heading: Study Tips for This Module
study_sections.content:
Although this is an introductory module, don't skip it — test questions are drawn from ALL parts of the syllabus, including this introduction.

Pay particular attention to:
• The five fundamental principles of British life
• The responsibilities you take on and the freedoms you receive
• The exact format of the Life in the UK test (number of questions, how to book, what to bring)
• The citizenship pledge

Everything you need to pass the test is in the official handbook. Read every section carefully.

key_facts: Test questions can be drawn from ALL parts of the handbook, including this introduction.
Becoming a permanent resident requires accepting UK responsibilities and respecting its laws, values and traditions.
The UK welcomes new migrants who add to the diversity and dynamism of national life.
memory_hook: This is an intro lesson - no memory hook needed. Just remember: questions come from the WHOLE test syllabus including this module!

supportive_messages.high_score: Great start! Now move on to the next lesson to learn the key facts! 🌟
